rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO APARTMENT ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleading
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 20, 2020 line 1. Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleading Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is OFF CALEDANR, moot. The matter addressed on the merits on Plaintiff's Motion for Judgment on the Petition. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN CAMPBELL ET AL VS. PACIFIC ARMS GOUGH STREET LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; First Appearance Fee *Paid* For Sun Property Management
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 20, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANTs ROBERT TRANTER, SARAH TRANTER, MARILYN ZERBE, BRENNAN ZERBE, TINA SHIH, JOHN SHIH, and SUN PROPERTY MANAGMENT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. There are triable issues of facts as to each of the three issues of which summary adjudication is requested. Further, as to the second issue, Defendant expressly excludes the cause of action for trespass to chattels from its request for Summary Adjudication. Finally, on the third issue as framed in the notice, no legal authority is cited and the entire argument appears in footnote 2 on page 22. = (501/CFH)
